Identification.LongTitle: An Act respecting pandemic prevention and preparedness

Provision.Text: This enactment enacts the Pandemic Prevention and Preparedness Act to require the Minister of Health to establish an advisory committee to review the response to the COVID-19 pandemic in Canada in order to reduce the risks associated with future pandemics and inform a pandemic prevention and preparedness plan.
It also requires the Minister of Health to establish, in consultation with other ministers, a pandemic prevention and preparedness plan, which is to include information provided by those ministers.

Finally, it amends the Department of Health Act to provide that the Minister of Health must appoint a national pandemic prevention and preparedness coordinator from among the officials of the Public Health Agency of Canada to coordinate the activities under the Pandemic Prevention and Preparedness Act.
Whereas the costs of prevention and preparedness measures are insignificant in comparison to the human and economic costs of a pandemic;
Whereas Parliament is committed to making efforts to prevent the risk of and prepare for future pandemics and to promote transparency and accountability in relation to those efforts;

Whereas it is critical to build on the lessons learned from previous outbreaks of serious diseases, including severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS), Ebola virus disease (EVD), Zika virus disease, tuberculosis, H1N1 flu and coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19);

Whereas a One Health approach â a multisectoral and multidisciplinary collaborative approach that focuses on the human, animal, plant and ecosystem health and welfare interface â is central to preventing the risk of future pandemics;
And whereas this approach requires sustained collaboration among various ministers, all levels of government and Indigenous communities;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Pandemic Prevention and Preparedness Act.
The purpose of this Act is to prevent the risk of and prepare for future pandemics and to promote transparency and accountability in relation to the Government of Canadaâs efforts to do so.

Heading.TitleText: Comprehensive Review of the COVID-19 Response

Subsection.Text: Within 90 days after the day on which this Act comes into force, the Minister of Health must establish an advisory committee to review the response to the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic in Canada.

The function of the advisory committee is to make recommendations for the improvement, throughout Canada, of preparedness efforts and response capabilities in relation to disease outbreaks in order to reduce the risks associated with future pandemics.
The committee must be gender-balanced and is to be composed of persons who reflect a range of relevant backgrounds and disciplines.
In conducting its review, the advisory committee is, among other things, to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: assess the capability of the Public Health Agency of Canada and the Department of Health to respond to the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic before and during the pandemic;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in collaboration with provincial and municipal governments, assess the public health and pandemic response capabilities of those governments;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: assess the effectiveness of the exercise of powers under any applicable federal laws before, during and after the pandemic and of the coordination of measures taken under those laws; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: analyse the health, economic and social factors relevant to the impact of the pandemic in Canada.

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Health must establish a pandemic prevention and preparedness plan.
The pandemic prevention and preparedness plan must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: set out a summary of mitigation strategies that the Minister of Health intends to implement in order to prevent the risk of and prepare for disease outbreaks that could lead to pandemics, as well as a projected timeline for their implementation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: identify the key drivers of pandemic risk and describe how Canadian activities, domestic and abroad, contribute to the risk;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: ensure sustained collaboration between the Minister of Health and provincial governments and Indigenous communities in the development of the plan in order to align approaches and address any jurisdictional challenges, including with respect to the collection and sharing of data;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide for training programs, including collaborative activities, with other levels of government, Indigenous communities and relevant agencies;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: ensure that standards and guidelines that pertain to the prevention of and response to pandemic risk are aligned with the approach described in paraÂ­graph (3)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: describe the state of research in relation to preventing and responding to infectious diseases that could lead to pandemics;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the establishment and interlinking of surveillance systems for infectious diseases in Canada and for infectious diseases of concern outside Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: support local public health and primary care capacity building;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: identify preparedness strategies for public health services across Canada including in respect of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the protection of vulnerable and marginalized populations,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: working conditions of essential workers across all sectors,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the availability and management of relevant stockpiles, including testing equipment and personal protective equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the surge capacity of human resources required for the testing and contact tracing of persons exposed to infectious diseases, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: communication of risk to the public;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: provide for measures, including training, to increase the ability of healthcare workers in a range of settings to manage sudden and unexpected increases in patient volume;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: include the following information, to be provided by the Minister of Industry:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the manufacturing capacity in Canada with respect to any product relevant to pandemic preparedness, including vaccines, testing equipment and personal protective equipment, and the measures that the Minister of Industry intends to take to address any supply chain gaps identified, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the communications capacity and infrastructure for electronic platforms and tools, including electronic applications that enable contact tracing of persons exposed to infectious diseases that could lead to pandemics;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: after consultation with the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food, the Minister of Industry and provincial governments, provide for measures to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: reduce the risks posed by antimicrobial resistance,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: regulate commercial activities that can contribute to pandemic risk, including industrial animal agriculture,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: promote commercial activities that can help reduce pandemic risk, including the production of alternative proteins, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: phase out commercial activities that disproportionately contribute to pandemic risk, including activities that involve high-risk species;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: include the following information, to be provided by the Minister of the Environment:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: after consultation with relevant provincial ministers, a summary of changes in land use in Canada, including in relation to disturbed habitats, that could contribute to pandemic risk, such as deforestation, encroachment on wildlife habitats and urbanization and that were made, in the case of the first plan, since the last report on changes in land use published under the Federal Sustainable Development Act or, in the case of the updated plans, during the reporting period for the updated plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a summary of the measures the Minister of the Environment intends to take to reduce the risk that the commercial wildlife trade in Canada and abroad will lead to a pandemic, including measures to regulate or phase out live animal markets, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a summary of the measures the Minister of the Environment intends to take to protect global biodiversity and to halt and reverse global deforestation;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: include a summary, to be provided by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, of the measures that that minister intends to take to support global health equity, including measures to increase public health capacity around the world and to ensure equitable access to vaccines, testing equipment and treatment;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: set out, in consultation with relevant ministers, a summary of key cooperative measures or agreements on disease outbreak prevention and preparedness between the Government of Canada, other foreign governments and key international organizations, including the World Health Organization, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Organization for Animal Health and the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations; and

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: set out, in consultation with the Minister of Transport, the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness and other relevant ministers, the routes by which and rates at which pathogens that could lead to pandemics could enter Canada and a summary of border-related measures that would be implemented to reduce any risk.

Subsection.Text: When establishing the plan under subsection (1) or when providing the required information under subsection (2), the Minister of Health or the ministers referred to in subsection (2), as the case may be, must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use a multisectoral and multidisciplinary collaborative approach, known as a One Health approach, that focuses on the human, animal, plant and ecosystem health and welfare interface;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: take into account the best scientific information available;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: leverage international expertise by using internationally developed metrics for pandemic prevention and preparedness, when applicable; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: take into account the recommendations made by the advisory committee following its review of the response to the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic in Canada.

Subsection.Text: Within two years after the day on which this Act comes into force, the Minister of Health must prepare a report setting out the plan and cause it to be tabled in each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after it is completed.
The Minister of Health must publish the report on the website of the Department of Health within 10 days after it has been tabled in both Houses of Parliament.

The Minister of Health must update the pandemic prevention and preparedness plan at least once every three years and cause a report setting out the updated plan to be tabled in each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after it is completed.
In the updated plan, the Minister of Health must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: report on the progress that has been made towards preventing pandemic risk and improving preparedness efforts in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in collaboration with the relevant ministers, provide any relevant updates on the information described under subsection 4(2); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: take into account the considerations set out in subsection 4(3).

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Health must publish the report on the website of the Department of Health within 10 days after it has been tabled in both Houses of Parliament.

Heading.TitleText: Amendment to the Department of Health Act

Section.Text: The Department of Health Act is amended by adding the following after section 4.1:

Section.MarginalNote: National coordinator â pandemic prevention and preparedness
Section.Text: For the purpose of coordinating the activities under the Pandemic Prevention and Preparedness Act, the Minister shall appoint a national pandemic prevention and preparedness coordinator from among the officials of the Public Health Agency of Canada and delegate to the coordinator the powers, duties and functions that the Minister considers appropriate.
